key_insight: A hybrid IFENN framework can learn phase-field fracture initiation and propagation on one benchmark geometry and generalize to unseen geometries by combining FEM with specialized neural surrogates for the coupled field.
engineering_relevance: This provides a faster offline-training route for fracture simulations while preserving physics-based accuracy, making phase-field fracture modeling more practical for complex engineering structures.
method: FEM solves mechanical equilibrium in the coupled mechanics problem.
DeepOKAN models the phase-field diffusion equation during crack initiation.
A CNN is used for the propagation stage, with artificial far-field boundary conditions to improve extrapolation.
evidence_signal: The paper reports physics-informed training on only a small number of increments and strategically sampled Gauss points, then evaluates the approach on both the training benchmark and unseen geometries.
watch_item: Generalization depends on careful handling of far-field extrapolation, so the artificial boundary conditions and training-point selection may be important to reproduce robustly.
paper_title: A hybrid IFENN solver for generalizable modeling of phase-field fracture initiation and propagation
